title: The Last Leaf – Concept Mastery Helper
author: O. Henry

concepts.lesson: The story shows how hope can change a person’s will to live. Johnsy regains the desire to recover when she sees the last leaf still on the creeper.
concepts.real_example: People often recover from illness faster when they maintain hope and positive thinking.

concepts.lesson: Sue’s care and support show the importance of true friendship. She tries her best to encourage Johnsy and keep her hopeful.
concepts.real_example: Friends often help us overcome difficult situations through encouragement and support.

concepts.lesson: Johnsy believes she will die when the last leaf falls. Her negative thinking worsens her condition.
concepts.real_example: People who lose hope may struggle to recover from illness or problems.

concepts.lesson: The doctor explains that medicine alone cannot cure Johnsy; she must also have the will to live.
concepts.real_example: Doctors often emphasize mental strength and positivity along with medical treatment.

concepts.lesson: Behrman dreams of painting a masterpiece throughout his life.
concepts.real_example: Many artists dream of creating a work that defines their career.

concepts.lesson: Behrman sacrifices his life by painting the leaf on a stormy night to save Johnsy’s hope.
concepts.real_example: People sometimes risk their lives to help others in times of need.

concepts.lesson: Behrman’s painted leaf becomes a symbol of hope and inspiration for Johnsy.
concepts.real_example: Art can inspire people and change their outlook on life.

concepts.lesson: Behrman’s painting becomes his masterpiece because it saves a life and inspires hope.
concepts.real_example: A masterpiece is not only about beauty but also about its impact on people.

concepts.lesson: Johnsy learns that life is precious and it is wrong to give up hope.
concepts.real_example: People often realise the importance of life after overcoming difficult situations.
